titleStmt.title: SII 25.71: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 71. (A.R. No. 71 of 1908.) VEḌĀL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara temple.

This inscription contains two fragmentary parts. The first piece refers to the king and his reign period. It seems to record the gift of four cows for a lamp to god Kaṟaikaṇṭīśuram-uḍaiyār of Viḍāl in Uttamaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Mūlaṅkiḻattinittāṇḍi. It was entrusted to the śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple.
The next piece records a gift of four cows for a perpetual lamp to the deity [Kaṟaika*]ṇḍīchchuram-uḍaiyār by a certain Periyāḷvi, daughter of a śeṭṭi, whose name is lost.
The other details are not clear as they have been indifferently engraved.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
cakkaravattikaḷ siri Irājātirāja tevaṟkku yāṇṭu 5 Āvatu Uttama co
ḻavaḷanāṭṭu viṭāl kaṟaikaṇṭi curamuṭaiyaṟkku mulaṅkiḻattinittāṇṭi vaitta caThis rest of lines are not traceable.
ṟukku viṭṭa pacu nāluṅ koṇṭu Ikkoyil kāṇiyuṭaiya civabrahmaṇan kāśyaThis rest of lines are not traceable.
ciṅkap paṉṉum gaṅgā
dharappaṉṉum IviruvoThis rest of lines are not traceable.

ṇṭiccaramuṭaiyāṟku-parivumai ceṭṭi makaḷ periyāḷvi vacca naṉtāviḷakkoṉ
ṟiṉāl pacu nālu §
Digital edition of SII 25.71 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
